rag_rules: Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Place range assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Relate outputs to inputs in component type guarantees without implementation-specific paths
Use bool type for comparison expressions and range checks
Delete clauses with unresolved references to AADL properties or unit tokens
generic_rules: Minimize repairs to only address validator diagnostics
Preserve existing architecture when fixing AGREE contracts
Use named constants for threshold values in range checks
Avoid implementation-specific paths in component type contracts
Delete invalid clauses rather than adding architecture to fix them
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq` for helper declarations and `const` for named constants
Format range checks as `eq name : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value`
Place assumptions and guarantees in the appropriate annex scope
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not add architecture to fix invalid clauses; delete them instead
Do not use implementation-specific identifiers in component type contracts
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AADL property names as AGREE variables
